findings.title: Reference to non-existent figure
findings.snippet: Figure~1 shows the relationship but I couldn't get the plotting software to work so you'll have to take my word for it.
findings.explanation: The text references "Figure 1" but there is no \figure environment, \includegraphics, or \label anywhere in the document. This is a dangling reference to content that does not exist.
findings.fix: Either add the actual figure with a proper \begin{figure} environment and \label, or remove the reference entirely.

findings.title: Run-on compound sentence (Figure 1 sentence)
findings.snippet: Figure~1 shows the relationship but I couldn't get the plotting software to work so you'll have to take my word for it.
findings.explanation: Three independent clauses are chained with "but" and "so" and no commas, producing a run-on sentence.
findings.fix: Figure~1 shows the relationship, but I couldn't get the plotting software to work, so you'll have to take my word for it.

findings.title: Missing commas around conditional clause
findings.snippet: Now if we set a minimum wage $\bar{w} > w^*$ then clearly
findings.explanation: A sentence-initial conditional clause ("if ... then") requires a comma after the introductory adverb and after the condition.
findings.fix: Now, if we set a minimum wage $\bar{w} > w^*$, then clearly

findings.title: Missing comma before 'where' relative clause
findings.snippet: demand $D(w) = b - cw$ where $w$ is the wage.
findings.explanation: A non-restrictive 'where' clause that defines notation should be set off by a comma.
findings.fix: demand $D(w) = b - cw$, where $w$ is the wage.

findings.title: Missing comma after sentence adverb and before conjunction
findings.snippet: Also Card and Krueger (1994) found the opposite but their methodology is super flawed in my opinion.
findings.explanation: The sentence adverb "Also" at the start of a sentence should be followed by a comma, and the coordinating conjunction "but" joining two independent clauses needs a preceding comma.
findings.fix: Also, Card and Krueger (1994) found the opposite, but their methodology is super flawed in my opinion.

findings.title: Missing comma after introductory element
findings.explanation: "OK" is an interjection and "so" is a transitional word; both need to be set off by commas.
